title: Add additional `userns` values to containers.conf.5
body:
I was suggested to set `containers.userns="nomap"` in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/26939#issuecomment-3242866375, but this value is only documented as allowed in `podman-run(1)` and `podman-systemd.unit(5)`, not `containers.conf(5)`. But `containers.userns="nomap"` seems to work as expected, so I've copied the documentation from https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/options/userns.container.md to here.

I have no idea what `private` does, so I've just left the current description as-is. If someone can tell me what it does, I can try and give it a description that explains how it's different from the other options.

comments.body:
@Luap99 

> thanks, you need to DCO sign off your commit https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs

Done.

> Also please also update the default containers.conf common/pkg/config/containers.conf to keep the docs in sync

Also done, thanks.

> I have no idea what private does, so I've just left the current description as-is. If someone can tell me what it does, I can try and give it a description that explains how it's different from the other options.

It creates a new private userns for the container, however it does not add any mappings. These would still have to be added via --uidmap for example (which already implies creating userns for the container so --userns=private is a bit pointless cli wise)

```
$ podman run --userns private quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 true
Error: must provide at least one UID or GID mapping to configure a user namespace
```

Though I guess for the config file It could make sense to not accidentally to create a a container without user namespace (host) though I doubt anyone is using that.

@Luap99 

> > I have no idea what private does, so I've just left the current description as-is. If someone can tell me what it does, I can try and give it a description that explains how it's different from the other options.
> 
> It creates a new private userns for the container, however it does not add any mappings. These would still have to be added via --uidmap for example (which already implies creating userns for the container so --userns=private is a bit pointless cli wise)

Ok thanks, I've updated the docs and repushed.
